req.title: System-Admin views org-announcement
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a org-announcement through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
Here's a comprehensive pytest test suite for the org-announcement viewing requirement:

```python
"""
Tests for system-admin viewing org-announcements through UI form.
Requirements covered:
- Authenticated system-admin can view org-announcement via UI form
- Mandatory fields are validated
- Audit record is written on successful view
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from uuid import uuid4


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def system_admin_user():
    """Return a fully authenticated system-admin user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = str(uuid4())
    user.username = "admin@example.com"
    user.role = "system-admin"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    user.is_active = True
    user.organization_id = str(uuid4())
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_admin_user():
    """Return an authenticated user WITHOUT system-admin role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = str(uuid4())
    user.username = "user@example.com"
    user.role = "org-member"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    user.is_active = True
    user.organization_id = str(uuid4())
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """Return an unauthenticated (anonymous) user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.username = None
    user.role = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    user.is_active = False
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def valid_announcement():
    """Return a fully populated org-announcement object."""
    announcement = MagicMock()
    announcement.id = str(uuid4())
    announcement.title = "Q3 Policy Update"
    announcement.body = "Please review the updated policies effective 1 Oct."
    announcement.organization_id = str(uuid4())
    announcement.created_by = str(uuid4())
    announcement.created_at = datetime(2024, 7, 1, 9, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    announcement.updated_at = datetime(2024, 7, 15, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    announcement.status = "published"
    announcement.priority = "normal"
    return announcement


@pytest.fixture
def announcement_missing_title(valid_announcement):
    """Return an announcement with a missing mandatory title field."""
    valid_announcement.title = None
    return valid_announcement


@pytest.fixture
def announcement_missing_body(valid_announcement):
    """Return an announcement with a missing mandatory body field."""
    valid_announcement.body = None
    return valid_announcement


@pytest.fixture
def announcement_missing_organization_id(valid_announcement):
    """Return an announcement with a missing mandatory organization_id field."""
    valid_announcement.organization_id = None
    return valid_announcement


@pytest.fixture
def announcement_missing_created_by(valid_announcement):
    """Return an announcement with a missing mandatory created_by field."""
    valid_announcement.created_by = None
    return valid_announcement


@pytest.fixture
def mock_announcement_service():
    """Return a mock announcement service."""
    with patch("services.announcement_service.AnnouncementService") as mock_cls:
        service = mock_cls.return_value
        yield service


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit_service():
    """Return a mock audit service."""
    with patch("services.audit_service.AuditService") as mock_cls:
        service = mock_cls.return_value
        yield service


@pytest.fixture
def mock_auth_service():
    """Return a mock authentication / authorisation service."""
    with patch("services.auth_service.AuthService") as mock_cls:
        service = mock_cls.return_value
        yield service


@pytest.fixture
def ui_form_view(mock_announcement_service, mock_audit_service, mock_auth_service):
    """
    Instantiate the UI form view under test, injecting mocked dependencies.
    Replace the import path to match your actual application structure.
    """
    from views.org_announcement_view import OrgAnnouncementView  # noqa: PLC0415
    return OrgAnnouncementView(
        announcement_service=mock_announcement_service,
        audit_service=mock_audit_service,
        auth_service=mock_auth_service,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _make_view_request(user, announcement_id):
    """Build a minimal request-like object for the view tests."""
    request = MagicMock()
    request.user = user
    request.method = "GET"
    request.path = f"/org-announcements/{announcement_id}/"
    request.META = {"REMOTE_ADDR": "192.168.1.100", "HTTP_USER_AGENT": "pytest-agent/1.0"}
    return request


# ===========================================================================
# 1. AUTHENTICATION TESTS
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuthentication:
    """Verify that only authenticated users can access the view."""

    def test_unauthenticated_user_is_redirected(
        self, ui_form_view, unauthenticated_user, valid_announcement
    ):
        """An unauthenticated request must NOT render the form; expect redirect/403."""
        request = _make_view_request(unauthenticated_user, valid_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert response.status_code in (302, 401, 403), (
            f"Expected redirect or auth error, got {response.status_code}"
        )

    def test_unauthenticated_user_does_not_trigger_audit(
        self, ui_form_view, unauthenticated_user, valid_announcement, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """No audit record should be written for an unauthenticated access attempt."""
        request = _make_view_request(unauthenticated_user, valid_announcement.id)

        ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        mock_audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_inactive_account_is_rejected(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement
    ):
        """A deactivated system-admin account must be refused access."""
        system_admin_user.is_active = False
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert response.status_code in (302, 401, 403)


# ===========================================================================
# 2. AUTHORISATION TESTS
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuthorisation:
    """Verify that only system-admin role can view org-announcements."""

    def test_non_admin_role_is_forbidden(
        self, ui_form_view, non_admin_user, valid_announcement, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """A user with a role other than system-admin must receive a 403."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(non_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert response.status_code == 403

    def test_non_admin_role_does_not_trigger_audit(
        self, ui_form_view, non_admin_user, valid_announcement,
        mock_announcement_service, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """No audit record should be created when a non-admin is rejected."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(non_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        mock_audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("forbidden_role", [
        "org-member", "viewer", "editor", "billing-admin", "support-agent", ""
    ])
    def test_all_non_admin_roles_are_forbidden(
        self, ui_form_view, non_admin_user, valid_announcement,
        mock_announcement_service, forbidden_role
    ):
        """Parametrised check: every non-system-admin role should be refused."""
        non_admin_user.role = forbidden_role
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(non_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert response.status_code == 403, (
            f"Role '{forbidden_role}' should be forbidden but got {response.status_code}"
        )

    def test_system_admin_role_is_permitted(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """A system-admin must receive a 200 OK."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert response.status_code == 200


# ===========================================================================
# 3. UI FORM RENDERING TESTS
# ===========================================================================

class TestFormRendering:
    """Verify that the UI form is correctly rendered for a valid request."""

    def test_form_renders_with_200_status(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """Successful view request returns HTTP 200."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_form_contains_announcement_title(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """The rendered form must include the announcement title."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert valid_announcement.title in str(response.content)

    def test_form_contains_announcement_body(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """The rendered form must include the announcement body."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert valid_announcement.body in str(response.content)

    def test_form_contains_organization_id(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """The rendered form must surface the organization_id."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert valid_announcement.organization_id in str(response.content)

    def test_form_contains_created_by(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """The rendered form must surface the created_by field."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert valid_announcement.created_by in str(response.content)

    def test_form_contains_status_field(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """The rendered form must surface the announcement status."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert valid_announcement.status in str(response.content)

    def test_form_uses_correct_template(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """The view must render using the designated template."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert response.template_name == "org_announcements/view.html"

    def test_form_is_readonly_for_view_action(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """The view form must be read-only (no editable inputs enabled)."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert response.context["form"].is_readonly is True

    def test_announcement_service_called_with_correct_id(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """The service layer must be queried with the exact announcement ID."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.assert_called_once_with(valid_announcement.id)


# ===========================================================================
# 4. MANDATORY FIELD VALIDATION TESTS
# ===========================================================================

class TestMandatoryFieldValidation:
    """
    Verify that the view correctly identifies and reports missing mandatory
    fields on the underlying announcement record.
    """

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("field_name,fixture_name", [
        ("title",           "announcement_missing_title"),
        ("body",            "announcement_missing_body"),
        ("organization_id", "announcement_missing_organization_id"),
        ("created_by",      "announcement_missing_created_by"),
    ])
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_returns_422(
        self, request, ui_form_view, system_admin_user,
        mock_announcement_service, field_name, fixture_name
    ):
        """
        Each missing mandatory field must cause the view to return 422
        Unprocessable Entity (or equivalent validation-error status).
        """
        incomplete_announcement = request.getfixturevalue(fixture_name)
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = incomplete_announcement
        http_request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, incomplete_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(http_request, announcement_id=incomplete_announcement.id)

        assert response.status_code == 422, (
            f"Missing '{field_name}' should yield 422, got {response.status_code}"
        )

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("field_name,fixture_name", [
        ("title",           "announcement_missing_title"),
        ("body",            "announcement_missing_body"),
        ("organization_id", "announcement_missing_organization_id"),
        ("created_by",      "announcement_missing_created_by"),
    ])
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_error_message_references_field(
        self, request, ui_form_view, system_admin_user,
        mock_announcement_service, field_name, fixture_name
    ):
        """Error response content must explicitly name the offending field."""
        incomplete_announcement = request.getfixturevalue(fixture_name)
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = incomplete_announcement
        http_request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, incomplete_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(http_request, announcement_id=incomplete_announcement.id)

        assert field_name in str(response.content).lower(), (
            f"Error message should reference the field '{field_name}'"
        )

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("field_name,fixture_name", [
        ("title",           "announcement_missing_title"),
        ("body",            "announcement_missing_body"),
        ("organization_id", "announcement_missing_organization_id"),
        ("created_by",      "announcement_missing_created_by"),
    ])
    def test_validation_failure_does_not_write_audit_record(
        self, request, ui_form_view, system_admin_user,
        mock_announcement_service, mock_audit_service, field_name, fixture_name
    ):
        """A failed validation MUST NOT produce an audit record."""
        incomplete_announcement = request.getfixturevalue(fixture_name)
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = incomplete_announcement
        http_request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, incomplete_announcement.id)

        ui_form_view.get(http_request, announcement_id=incomplete_announcement.id)

        mock_audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_all_mandatory_fields_present_passes_validation(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """When all mandatory fields are present the view renders successfully."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_nonexistent_announcement_returns_404(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """Requesting an announcement that does not exist must return 404."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = None
        nonexistent_id = str(uuid4())
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, nonexistent_id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=nonexistent_id)

        assert response.status_code == 404

    def test_nonexistent_announcement_does_not_write_audit(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, mock_announcement_service, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """No audit record on a 404 outcome."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = None
        nonexistent_id = str(uuid4())
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, nonexistent_id)

        ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=nonexistent_id)

        mock_audit_service.write.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 5. AUDIT RECORD TESTS
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditRecord:
    """Verify that a correct audit record is written on a successful view."""

    def test_audit_record_is_written_on_success(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement,
        mock_announcement_service, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """A successful view must call the audit service exactly once."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        mock_audit_service.write.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_contains_actor_id(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement,
        mock_announcement_service, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """Audit record must identify the acting user by ID."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.write.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("actor_id") == system_admin_user.id

    def test_audit_record_contains_action_type(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement,
        mock_announcement_service, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """Audit record must record the action as VIEW_ORG_ANNOUNCEMENT."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.write.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("action") == "VIEW_ORG_ANNOUNCEMENT"

    def test_audit_record_contains_resource_id(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement,
        mock_announcement_service, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """Audit record must reference the specific announcement viewed."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.write.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("resource_id") == valid_announcement.id

    def test_audit_record_contains_resource_type(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement,
        mock_announcement_service, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """Audit record must identify the resource type as OrgAnnouncement."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.write.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("resource_type") == "OrgAnnouncement"

    def test_audit_record_contains_outcome_success(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement,
        mock_announcement_service, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """Audit record outcome must be SUCCESS."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.write.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("outcome") == "SUCCESS"

    def test_audit_record_contains_timestamp(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement,
        mock_announcement_service, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """Audit record must carry a timestamp close to the time of the request."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)
        before = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

        ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        after = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.write.call_args
        timestamp = kwargs.get("timestamp")
        assert timestamp is not None
        assert before <= timestamp <= after

    def test_audit_record_contains_ip_address(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement,
        mock_announcement_service, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """Audit record must capture the requester's IP address."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.write.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("ip_address") == "192.168.1.100"

    def test_audit_record_written_after_successful_render(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement,
        mock_announcement_service, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """
        The audit write must happen AFTER the form is successfully rendered
        (i.e., service retrieval precedes audit write).
        """
        call_order = []
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.side_effect = (
            lambda *a, **kw: call_order.append("get") or valid_announcement
        )
        mock_audit_service.write.side_effect = (
            lambda *a, **kw: call_order.append("audit")
        )
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert call_order == ["get", "audit"], (
            f"Expected ['get', 'audit'], got {call_order}"
        )

    def test_audit_write_failure_does_not_hide_successful_response(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement,
        mock_announcement_service, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """
        If the audit service raises an internal error the view should still
        return the rendered content (or a graceful error), and must not silently
        swallow the audit failure without logging it.
        """
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        mock_audit_service.write.side_effect = Exception("Audit store unavailable")
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        # The view should either succeed with 200 or return a 5xx — it must NOT
        # silently succeed and silently drop the audit record.
        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert response.status_code in (200, 500, 503), (
            "View must handle audit failure gracefully"
        )
        mock_audit_service.write.assert_called_once()


# ===========================================================================
# 6. EDGE CASES & SECURITY
# ===========================================================================

class TestEdgeCasesAndSecurity:
    """Additional boundary and security-oriented tests."""

    def test_http_post_method_not_allowed(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """The view-only form must refuse POST requests (read-only resource)."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)
        request.method = "POST"

        response = ui_form_view.post(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert response.status_code == 405

    def test_malformed_announcement_id_returns_400(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """A syntactically invalid announcement ID must return 400."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.side_effect = ValueError("Invalid UUID")
        malformed_id = "not-a-valid-uuid!!!"
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, malformed_id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=malformed_id)

        assert response.status_code == 400

    def test_malformed_id_does_not_write_audit(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, mock_announcement_service, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """No audit record on a bad-request outcome."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.side_effect = ValueError("Invalid UUID")
        malformed_id = "not-a-valid-uuid!!!"
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, malformed_id)

        ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=malformed_id)

        mock_audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_sql_injection_in_id_is_rejected(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """An SQL-injection payload in the ID parameter must not reach the DB."""
        injection_id = "' OR '1'='1"
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.side_effect = ValueError("Invalid UUID")
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, injection_id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=injection_id)

        assert response.status_code in (400, 422)
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.assert_called_once_with(injection_id)

    def test_announcement_from_different_org_is_forbidden(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement, mock_announcement_service
    ):
        """
        A system-admin must not view an announcement belonging to a
        different organisation.
        """
        valid_announcement.organization_id = str(uuid4())  # different org
        system_admin_user.organization_id = str(uuid4())    # admin's own org
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)

        response = ui_form_view.get(request, announcement_id=valid_announcement.id)

        assert response.status_code == 403

    def test_multiple_rapid_requests_each_produce_one_audit_record(
        self, ui_form_view, system_admin_user, valid_announcement,
        mock_announcement_service, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """Each successful view call must produce exactly one audit entry."""
        mock_announcement_service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_announcement
        request = _make_view_request(system_admin_user, valid_announcement.id)
